slides.title: A cold shower beats your *morning coffee*.
slides.sub: Done at the right moment, it hands you hours of clean alertness. Done at the wrong one, you waste it — or worse.

slides.title: Cold lifts your chemistry
slides.body: Brief cold raises *dopamine and noradrenaline*, which may support hours of clean alertness and a steadier mood afterward.

slides.title: Use it in the morning
slides.body: Cold gives its biggest lift *early in the day*, so save it for when you actually want to feel switched on.

slides.title: Not right after lifting
slides.body: Cold straight after a workout can *blunt muscle growth*. Wait a few hours if building strength is the goal.

slides.title: Keep it short
slides.body: You don't need an ice bath. *30 to 90 seconds* of cold at the end of a shower is plenty.

slides.title: Control the exhale
slides.body: Don't gasp. *Slow, long exhales* keep you calm under the cold and help your body settle into it.

slides.title: Trade the jitters for *clean energy*.
slides.body: Log cold showers as a habit in Vibe Health and watch how the mornings you do it move your daily score and mood.

caption:
A cold shower can beat your morning coffee — but only if you time it right. 🚿

Use it in the morning, not right after lifting, keep it to 30–90 seconds, and control your exhale instead of gasping. Save this. 🔖

Log it as a habit in Vibe Health and watch it move your daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: The Right Way to Use Cold Showers for Energy
